key_insight: A contact-driven isogeometric FEM updating framework can recover spatially varying nonlinear material properties from surface displacements and contact forces.
engineering_relevance: This enables non-destructive identification of heterogeneous soft materials and tissues, supporting biomechanics, material testing, and inverse design under large deformation contact.
method: Use full-field displacement data from the free surface, and contact forces when available.
Represent material-property fields with low-order Lagrange interpolation separate from the IGA mesh.
Minimize a least-squares FEMU objective with trust-region reflective optimization, aided by analytical derivatives and material continuation.
evidence_signal: The method was validated on three synthetic contact problems: a Canham shell strip, an abdominal-wall shell model, and a Neo-Hookean block, demonstrating reconstruction of inhomogeneous material fields.
watch_item: Performance depends on good measurements and local optimization, so identifiability, initialization, and noise sensitivity should be checked carefully in practical experiments.
paper_title: Contact-based inverse analysis for nonlinear material identification in spatially heterogeneous solids
